summary:
The marker that increases in patients with hepatitis B infection is HBsAg because it indicates initial viral activity before any other antibodies develop; it persists throughout acute infections but can also indicate long-term conditions if not resolved within about half a year after detection. ```plaintext
The marker that increases in patients with hepatitis B infection is HBsAg, indicating early viral activity which continues during both acute and potential chronic phases of the disease.
```

The marker that increases in patients with hepatitis B infection is HBsAg, indicating early viral activity which continues during both acute and potential chronic phases of the disease.
```. Answer choice: B
